accepted.reason: Compact tax-bracket kernel plus repeated vector fill offers clean control-flow examples.
Chunk extraction, row/column statistics, and cyclic tensor shifts are strong array-processing kernels.
Grouped ranking metric computation is a useful evaluation-loop kernel for ML workflows.
Bidirectional quality trimming algorithm is a practical sequence-processing routine.
Velocity-constraint matrix construction is a numerically meaningful robotics/trajectory kernel.
Quartic/Gaussian basis and integrated basis functions are reusable mesh-based force kernels.
Instruction parsing and repeated permutation transforms provide compact discrete-state update logic.

skipped.reason: Cython language feature regression tests, not standalone algorithmic kernels.
Mostly placeholder stubs with pass statements and no substantive kernel logic.
Heavily domain-specific parsing/grid generation tied to project data format and assumptions.
